Tests if the prototype of an Object instance has Object as its constructor.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion that checks the prototype chain of a newly created object in JavaScript. It verifies that the constructor of the prototype of an object created with `Object()` is `Object`. This assertion ensures that the object follows the expected prototype inheritance pattern in JavaScript, where the default constructor for an object is the `Object` constructor. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's object prototype behavior.
